access_control: Role-scoped: Security agent retrieves Security determinations; Legal agent retrieves Legal determinations; Procurement agent retrieves Procurement determinations.
retrieval_note: Records are supplementary context only — precedents inform but do not override current policy documents. Where a precedent conflicts with current policy, current policy takes precedence.

records.key_issue: EU employee personal data in scope — DPA required
records.product_summary: Workforce productivity analytics. Ingests shift-level attendance records and productivity metrics for EU-based plant employees (Dortmund and Kraków). Nightly structured export from Lichen workforce management system. No ERP integration. No persistent vendor system credentials.

key_questionnaire_facts.data_subjects: EU-based Lichen manufacturing employees (~340 individuals, two facilities)

it_security.determination: No ERP integration. Data classification: REGULATED (EU personal data). Fast-track ineligible. Security review complete. No material findings.

legal.determination: GDPR Art. 28 DPA required. EU employee personal data confirmed in scope. DPA executed on Lichen standard terms. SCCs not required — data residency confirmed AWS eu-central-1; no EEA transfer.

procurement.determination: Standard approval path. T2 value. No executive approval required. Fast-track ineligible — regulated data in scope.

precedents_established: EU employee operational data (shift records, attendance, productivity metrics) = personal data. GDPR Art. 28 DPA required regardless of integration architecture.
Data residency within EEA (AWS eu-central-1) — SCCs not required.
NDA confirmed executed before submission — no NDA blocker, legal review proceeds without delay.
No ERP integration — no tier classification step required.
records.lessons_recorded: Reviewer note (2023-09-14): DPA trigger was data type, not integration method. EU employee records alone were sufficient — no ERP access needed to trigger Art. 28. NDA pre-confirmed by vendor; zero delay on legal phase. SOC 2 Type II (Big Four, unqualified) accepted for ISP-001 §12.1.3 without additional review.

records.key_issue: Ambiguous ERP integration description — tier unclassified — PROVISIONAL — resolved via architecture diagram

vendor.name: Fortbridge Systems, Inc.
vendor.final_status: APPROVED — PROVISIONAL resolved after tier assignment

records.product_summary: Predictive maintenance and asset management. Requires SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) module data — equipment utilization and maintenance schedules. Fortbridge Connector Agent installed in Lichen environment; syncs PM data on a schedule to Fortbridge cloud. No EU personal data in scope.

key_questionnaire_facts.erp_integration_description: Fortbridge Connector Agent installed in Lichen environment. Synchronizes PM data on a scheduled basis to Fortbridge cloud. Vendor states agent does not hold persistent SAP credentials but acknowledges connector may operate with a dedicated service account depending on customer SAP configuration.

provisional_determination.triggered_by: Questionnaire Q2.2 ambiguous. Could not confirm Tier 2 (mediated) vs Tier 3 (indirect/file-based). Possible dedicated service account reference introduced credential ownership ambiguity.
information_requested: Architecture diagram — Fortbridge Connector Agent authentication method
Confirmation: does agent hold SAP service account credentials or invoke Lichen-managed credential broker?
List of SAP PM data objects accessed

provisional_resolution.resolution_evidence: Architecture diagram received. Connector Agent runs as scheduled task in Lichen environment. Uses Lichen-managed SAP service account scoped to PM module read-only objects via HashiCorp Vault. Agent holds no credentials independently.
provisional_resolution.tier_assignment_rationale: Lichen-controlled agent process. Lichen-managed credentials. Fortbridge cloud receives extracted data only — no SAP access.
provisional_resolution.penetration_test: Waived. SOC 2 Type II accepted as compensating control for Tier 2.

initial.determination: ERP integration confirmed. Tier: UNCLASSIFIED / PENDING REVIEW. Architecture diagram requested. Onboarding PROVISIONAL.

final.determination: Tier assigned: Tier 2 — Mediated. Lichen-managed credentials confirmed. Fortbridge holds no SAP service account. SOC 2 Type II accepted as compensating control.

legal.determination: No EU personal data in scope. DPA not required. NDA confirmed executed. Legal review complete.

procurement.determination: Class A / T3: STANDARD — ENHANCED path. Fast-track ineligible. CPO sign-off obtained. Held pending IT Security tier resolution.

precedents_established: Ambiguous ERP integration questionnaire response — grounds for PROVISIONAL, not denial.
Tier assignment turns on credential ownership and presence of a Lichen-controlled mediation layer.
Vendor agent invoking Lichen-managed credentials at runtime (via secrets management) — satisfies Tier 2 (Mediated).
Vendor agent holding own service account with persistent SAP session — satisfies Tier 1 (Direct); higher approval threshold applies.
Tier 2 assignment requires architecture diagram review and CISO sign-off.
PROVISIONAL IT Security status does not block Legal or Procurement reviews from proceeding in parallel.
records.lessons_recorded: Reviewer note (2023-06-03): Targeted architecture diagram request (three specific questions) returned usable response in 6 business days vs. open-ended requests which historically return incomplete answers. Legal and Procurement ran in parallel during PROVISIONAL window with no rework required once tier was assigned.

records.key_issue: NDA 'in progress' at submission — information-exchange phase blocked 23 days

vendor.name: Nexus Data Partners, LLC
vendor.final_status: APPROVED — delayed 23 days pending NDA confirmation

records.product_summary: Supplier risk intelligence platform. Ingests Lichen supplier master data and contract metadata — supplier contact names, email addresses, contract values, payment terms, commodity codes. Structured exports from Lichen procurement system. No ERP integration.

key_questionnaire_facts.data_subjects: Supplier contacts — globally distributed; subset EU-based individuals
key_questionnaire_facts.nda_status_as_stated_by_vendor: In progress — Nexus legal team has reviewed Lichen's standard NDA and is coordinating internally for execution. We expect to return a countersigned copy within the week.

nda_timeline.followup_2_result: Vendor reported internal approvals still in progress

it_security.determination: No ERP integration. Regulated data (EU supplier contact personal data). Fast-track ineligible. Security review complete. No material findings.

initial.determination: GDPR Art. 28 DPA required — EU supplier contact personal data confirmed. DPA negotiation initiated. NDA: PROVISIONAL — not confirmed executed. Information-exchange phase BLOCKED per ISP-001 §12.1.4.

final.determination: NDA confirmed executed 2022-12-07. DPA executed 2022-12-19. Hold lifted. Legal review complete.

procurement.determination: Standard path. T2. Fast-track ineligible — regulated data in scope, NDA unconfirmed at submission. Onboarding record flagged: data exchange blocked pending NDA.

precedents_established: NDA status 'in progress' or 'expected shortly' does not satisfy ISP-001 §12.1.4. Confirmed execution required.
Information-exchange phase formally blocked until NDA execution confirmed by Legal.
Domain reviews and DPA negotiation may proceed in parallel during NDA confirmation gap.
Both NDA and DPA must be confirmed executed before any data exchange — including samples or test files.
Business sponsor must be explicitly notified when data exchange is blocked.
records.lessons_recorded: Reviewer note (2023-03-08): 23-day delay attributable entirely to NDA gap. Vendor's 'within the week' estimate was not met; two follow-ups required before formal hold notice issued. DPA negotiation ran in parallel with no rework. Gap would have been avoided if NDA execution had been confirmed before questionnaire submission.

records.key_issue: All fast-track conditions satisfied — streamlined path — 4-business-day completion

vendor.name: Clearline Software, Inc.
fast_track_eligible_conditions_met: NDA confirmed executed (pilot NDA, confirmed by Legal)
Prior Lichen engagement — 90-day pilot Q1 2023, no findings
No open PROVISIONAL or ESCALATED items

records.product_summary: Standalone process documentation and workflow diagramming SaaS. No Lichen system integration. SSO login only. No Lichen data ingested, exported, or processed by vendor. User-created content only.

key_questionnaire_facts.nda_source: Pilot agreement NDA — confirmed carried over by Legal
key_questionnaire_facts.prior_engagement_description: 90-day pilot, Lichen Process Engineering team, Q1 2023. No security or compliance issues identified.

it_security.determination: Notification only — Class C / T1. No system integration. No regulated data. Notified for inventory purposes. No review required.

legal.determination: No DPA required — no personal data processed by vendor. NDA confirmed executed (pilot). Standard confidentiality provisions in order.

procurement.determination: STREAMLINED path. All fast-track conditions satisfied. CPO approved. Contract executed. Complete.

precedents_established: Fast-track achievable when all seven conditions in PROC-MATRIX-001 §5 are met.
Completed prior engagement (pilot or prior contract) satisfies the prior-relationship condition for fast-track.
Class C / T1, no regulated data — IT Security notification only, not review. Not a waiver; correct risk-level response.
Streamlined path can complete in under 5 business days when all prerequisites confirmed at submission.
records.lessons_recorded: Reviewer note (2023-11-02): 4-day completion. Prior pilot was the enabling factor — without it, first-time vendor rule would have defaulted engagement to standard path per PROC-MATRIX-001 §5. IT Security notification documented in audit log. No rework at any stage.
